title: Telegram node hangs due to broken IPv6 routing
symptom: The Telegram node in n8n gets stuck in Testing / Loading / Executing and eventually times out without explicit errors.
not_this_if: No changes made to the Telegram API keys
root_cause: The issue arises from broken IPv6 routing, which causes Node.js to prefer IPv6 addresses. This results in a TLS handshake that hangs when trying to connect to the Telegram API, leading to indefinite execution times.
fix_steps: Set the environment variable NODE_OPTIONS to '--dns-result-order=ipv4first'
agent_instruction: Set NODE_OPTIONS to prioritize IPv4 and restart n8n.
